titleStmt.title: Letter from Alfred Beckett to Nicholas Lamb, [May 1916]
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Alfred Beckett

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Alfred Beckett to Nicholas Lamb enclosing a list of cases provided by the commissioner of valuations that need reviewed by the Longford Urban District Council. Alfred Beckett (b. 1864) was a civil servant who served as Chief Clerk in the General Valuation Office of Ireland. Nicholas Lamb (b. 1861) was the Town Clerk of Longford and served on the Longford Urban District Council. The Longford Urban District Council was the towns local authority and was renamed the Longford Town Council in 2002.

correspAction.placeName: Longford Urban District Council, Longford, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
General Valuation of Ireland
6 Ely Place Dublin Sir I am directed by the Commissioner of
Valuation to enclose a list of cases requiring
revision which have been furnished direct to him or
have otherwise come under the notice of this Department,
and to request that should your Council consider
such revisions necessary you will include the
cases in the lists which you are to forward here
by the end of the present month in accordance
with the provisions of the Valuation Acts. You
you will observe that, by section n of the Act 17 Vic
cap 8 these lists should come through the hands of
the rate of Collection of the District I am Sir
Your Obdt. Servant
Alfred Beckett
Chief Clerk address Urban Dist. Council
Longford
